name: Gateron KS-27 Low-Profile Optical Switch
summary: Low-profile optical switch used in Keychron K1 SE — very limited individual availability
pinDescription: No electrical pins — same PCB-mounted IR design as full-size Gateron optical. Alignment posts only.
keycapMountNote: Gateron low-profile proprietary stem — NOT compatible with Choc or MX keycaps
hotswapNote: Proprietary Gateron low-profile optical hot-swap. Not cross-compatible with anything else.
scanningNote: Same PCB-mounted IR design as full-size Gateron optical. See gateron-optical-full design notes.
footprintNote: No official KiCad footprint. Dimensions are estimated. Would need reverse-engineering from Keychron K1 SE optical PCB.
designNotes: Very limited individual availability — mostly available as Keychron spare parts.
Proprietary keycap mount — NOT Choc-compatible, NOT MX-compatible.
Finding compatible keycaps is extremely difficult outside of Keychron's ecosystem.
Same PCB-mounted IR architecture as full-size Gateron optical — adds complexity to PCB design.
No official footprint or datasheet — all dimensions are approximate.
Consider Kailh Choc v1 (mechanical, widely available, good ecosystem) for a more practical low-profile option.
features: Contactless optical sensing — long lifespan, no debounce
Reduced travel distance (2.5mm) for faster key presses
concerns: VERY limited availability — mostly Keychron spare parts
Proprietary keycap stem — almost no compatible aftermarket keycaps
No official datasheet, footprint, or pin dimensions
Same optical PCB complexity as full-size variant
For practical low-profile builds, Kailh Choc v1 (mechanical) is a far better choice
